turns.user_message: I used to breeze through my days without a second thought, but lately everything feels so heavy. I catch myself holding my breath over the smallest little things, like waiting for a notification or hearing an unfamiliar sound. It is frustrating because I know it is just my own head playing tricks on me. I try to shake it off and pretend I am fine, but the tension just sits there in my chest. I really miss how easy it used to be to just relax and let things slide.

I remember when I could actually look forward to the weekend, but now I just brace myself for whatever comes next. The worry has been building up for months, turning into this constant background noise that I cannot seem to mute. I find myself pacing around my room and checking the locks over and over again. It is exhausting to carry this invisible weight around when I know I should just be able to breathe normally. I keep hoping the old version of me will just snap back into place, but nothing changes.

I used to laugh off little surprises, but lately every unexpected sound sends my heart racing straight through my ribs. This relentless cycle has been dragging on for so long that I barely recognize my own reflection anymore. My hands shake without warning and my thoughts just spin faster than I can catch them. I am completely drained from trying to force myself back into that calm person I used to be. It feels like I am trapped inside my own head and I have no idea how to finally stop the spinning.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This has been going on for a while.
